title: Ramadan Month
promptText: Which Islamic month is fasting in?
explanation: Ramadan is the month in which the Quran was revealed and fasting becomes obligatory.

title: Why Fast
promptText: Complete the verse idea: fasting was prescribed so that you may become ___.
explanation: The goal of fasting is taqwa, not hunger alone.

title: Laylat al-Qadr
promptText: Which night is better than one thousand months?
explanation: Laylat al-Qadr is one of the greatest nights of the year.

title: Finish the Ayah
promptText: Finish the ayah: 'And We have certainly made the Quran easy for ___.’
explanation: The Quran repeatedly invites people to remember and reflect.

title: Iftar Time
promptText: When does the daily fast end?
explanation: The fast ends at Maghrib when the sun sets.

title: Suhur
promptText: What is the pre-dawn meal before fasting called?
explanation: Suhur is a blessed meal before the fast begins.

title: Ramadan Practices
promptText: Match the Ramadan term to its meaning.
explanation: These are core terms every learner should know during Ramadan.

title: Ramadan Order
promptText: Tap these Ramadan moments in the correct order for one fasting day.
answerText: Suhur, Fajr, fasting day, Maghrib iftar.
explanation: This teaches the daily rhythm of fasting.

title: Zakat al-Fitr
promptText: Which charity is given before the Eid al-Fitr prayer?
explanation: It helps the poor share in the joy of Eid.

title: Eid Prayer
promptText: How many rak'ahs is the Eid prayer?
explanation: The Eid prayer is a short communal prayer followed by a khutbah.
